subject: FW: For Mr. Jeffrey Epstein-JUNO SCREENING
body:
From: Peggy Siegal [mailto:peggy@peggysiegal.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, December 11, 2007 5:09 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Mr. Jeffrey Epstein-JUNO SCREENING

 

 
<http://rs6.net/on.jsp?t=1101908000453.0.1101430287919.1189&ts=S0305&o=h
ttp://ui.constantcontact.com/images/p1x1.gif> 

If you have already replied to this email we are sorry and please
disregard.

 

 

FOX SEARCHLIGHT PICTURES

 

CORDIALLY INVITES YOU TO JOIN

ELLEN PAGE   JASON REITMAN

 

 

 AT A PRIVATE SCREENING OF

 

JUNO

 

AUDIENCE AWARD, 2007 ST. LOUIS INTERNATIONAL FILM FESTIVAL

AUDIENCE AWARD, 2007 AUSTIN FILM FESTIVAL

BEST FILM AWARD, 2007 ROME FILM FESTIVAL

 

STARRING

ELLEN PAGE   MICHAEL CERA

ALLISON JANNEY   J.K. SIMMONS   OLIVIA THIRLBY

RAINN WILSON WITH JASON BATEMAN AND JENNIFER GARNER

 

DIRECTED BY JASON REITMAN

 

WRITTEN BY DIABLO CODY 

 

THURSDAY, DECEMBER 13, 2007

 

7:00 PM CINEMA 3

1001 THIRD AVENUE AT 60TH STREET

 

www.THEjunoVERSE.com
<http://rs6.net/tn.jsp?e=001S7i4syjAEmDG48kcq0dUzGsuIPykrd1SPSQqRgXW3uB1
8bskarr5jiwqGnHxzVlpjQyObheEyrasR1Bu2SnBEMl9fP0gk-tDa4AVbkDjP9UuCLDj_zWu
sw==> 

 

JUNO stars Ellen Page as the title character, a whip-smart teen
confronting an unplanned pregnancy by her classmate Bleeker (Michael
Cera).  With the help of her best friend Leah (Olivia Thirlby), Juno
finds her unborn child a "perfect" set of parents: an affluent suburban
couple, Mark and Vanessa (Jason Bateman and Jennifer Garner), longing to
adopt. Luckily, Juno has the total support of her parents (J.K. Simmons
and Allison Janney) as she faces some tough decisions, flirts with
adulthood and ultimately figures out where she belongs.

 

 

"Juno" has an underlying theme, a message that is not anti-abortion but
rather pro-adulthood. It follows its heroine - and by the end she has
earned that title - on a twisty path toward responsibility and greater
self-understanding." - A.O Scott, NEW YORK TIMES

 

"Credit the gifted Jason Reitman, screenwriter Diablo Cody and the
sensational lead actress Ellen Page and a near flawless cast for making
'JUNO' the feel-good movie of the season." - Jack Mathews, NEW YORK
DAILY NEWS 

"A fresh, quirky unusually intelligent comedy with dialogue so quick and
funny you feel the actors are performing it on a high-wire." - Roger
Ebert, CHICAGO SUN TIMES

"Ms. Page handles Juno's artful eccentricities and motor-mouth dialogue
with a light touch, adding a crucial hint of self-consciousness that
keeps her from being precious. Juno is endearing because Mr. Page
doesn't try to make her so. This is a wonderfully confident comic
performance, sturdy and sure, with a barely perceptible underlay of
sadness and worry." - Karen Durbin, THE NEW YORK TIMES

 

 

 

 

 
<http://visitor.constantcontact.com/d.jsp?p=un&v=0016DNjNiidDhR5-hLIXFRF
oKynI9wK9QlvRLRPgLQitY2qAoVjwV5Fp1depAVL3n9c2VmWUWjsZG6cf2BXtwnRRg%3D%3D
> 

This email was sent to lesley@nysgllc.com, by peggy@peggysiegal.com

Peggy Siegal | 4 West 58th Street | mezzanine | New York | NY | 10019

1001 THIRD AVENUE AT 60TH STREET

 

www.THEjunoVERSE.com

 

JUNO stars Ellen Page as the title character, a whip-smart teen confronting an unplanned pregnancy by her classmate Bleeker (Michael Cera).  With the help of her best friend Leah (Olivia Thirlby), Juno finds her unborn child a “perfect” set of parents: an affluent suburban couple, Mark and Vanessa (Jason Bateman and Jennifer Garner), longing to adopt. Luckily, Juno has the total support of her parents (J.K. Simmons and Allison Janney) as she faces some tough decisions, flirts with adulthood and ultimately figures out where she belongs.

 

 

"Juno" has an underlying theme, a message that is not anti-abortion but rather pro-adulthood. It follows its heroine — and by the end she has earned that title — on a twisty path toward responsibility and greater self-understanding." - A.O Scott, NEW YORK TIMES

 

"Credit the gifted Jason Reitman, screenwriter Diablo Cody and the sensational lead actress Ellen Page and a near flawless cast for making ‘JUNO’ the feel-good movie of the season." - Jack Mathews, NEW YORK DAILY NEWS

"A fresh, quirky unusually intelligent comedy with dialogue so quick and funny you feel the actors are performing it on a high-wire." – Roger Ebert, CHICAGO SUN TIMES

"Ms. Page handles Juno’s artful eccentricities and motor-mouth dialogue with a light touch, adding a crucial hint of self-consciousness that keeps her from being precious. Juno is endearing because Mr. Page doesn’t try to make her so. This is a wonderfully confident comic performance, sturdy and sure, with a barely perceptible underlay of sadness and worry." – Karen Durbin, THE NEW YORK TIMES
